mnemonics: ARGC-V Counts: ArgC Counts the number of arguments (including the program name)
ARGV-V Vector: ArgV is a Vector (array) of strings containing each argument
keyPoints: The main() function in a C program can take two arguments: int argc and char *argv[]
argc (argument count) is an integer representing the number of command-line arguments passed
argv (argument vector) is an array of character pointers (strings) listing all the arguments
commonMistakes: Forgetting that argv[0] is the program name, so user arguments start at argv[1]
Not checking the value of argc before accessing argv elements, leading to segmentation faults
